title: Recover A Stuck AGV By Using Go-to To Send It To The Hospital Drop-off Area
summary: Source-specific recovery example from training: when a tipper goes down and an AGV will not leave, the slide instructs the user to remove and add the AGV, then use the Go-to function to send it to the hospital drop-off area. The AGV is expected to pick up a new task once its tote has been removed.
when_to_use: Use this source-described recovery example when a tipper disruption leaves an AGV stuck and the AGV will not leave, and the interface provides the AGV selection and Go-to controls needed to send the AGV to the hospital drop-off area.
not_for: Do not use as a complete remove-and-add procedure; the source references that action but does not define how to perform it.
Do not use if the hospital drop-off square cannot be identified from the interface or local documentation.
Do not use as a general AGV removal authorization; the source packet does not define permissions or approval requirements for remove/add.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform the source-described AGV selection and Go-to recovery actions when available in the interface.
Escalate when the remove/add action, hospital drop-off square, or post-drop-off recovery outcome is not available or not understood from the source.

safety_notes: This runbook is based on a training example and is incomplete for the remove-and-add portion; use SME review before operational reliance.
The source does not define the exact remove-and-add procedure for the AGV.
Escalate if required controls are unavailable or unclear in the interface.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the robot or object selection interface
Go to function in the interface
Knowledge of the hospital drop-off square
Ability to remove the tote from the AGV

access_required: Access to the robot or object selection interface
Permission to use the Go to function
system_preconditions: The AGV involved can be identified in the robot or object selection view.
The hospital drop-off square is known to the user.
The source example condition applies: a tipper goes down and an AGV will not leave.

visual_references.description: Training slide showing the Robot Selection Go-to recovery example, including AGV selection, Go to, square selection, and Confirm.

steps.title: Select the AGV in the object selection view
steps.purpose: Identify the affected AGV and display its AGV information before recovery actions.
steps.instruction: In the robot or object selection view, select the AGV involved so its AGV information is displayed.
steps.expected_result: The selected AGV is highlighted or otherwise selected in the interface and AGV information is shown.
steps.healthy_condition: AGV information is displayed after selecting the AGV object.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV cannot be identified or selected, or AGV information does not appear after selection.

actions.label: Select AGV object
actions.exact_action: Select the AGV involved so its AGV information is displayed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: AGV object selection behavior and AGV information shown after selecting the AGV.

stop_or_escalate_if: The AGV cannot be located in the robot or object selection view.
Selecting the AGV does not display AGV information.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript states the user selects the object, and under AGV, selecting it will show AGV information.

steps.title: Remove and add the AGV if following the recovery example
steps.purpose: Apply the source-described prerequisite recovery action referenced in the example before using Go-to.
steps.instruction: If using the source example recovery, remove and add the AGV as stated on the slide before using Go-to.
steps.expected_result: The AGV has been removed and added back as required by the source example.
steps.healthy_condition: The remove-and-add action is completed using an already known and authorized method.
steps.failure_condition: The remove-and-add method is not known, not available, or not authorized from the available interface and procedures.

actions.label: Remove and add AGV
actions.exact_action: Remove and add the AGV as stated on the slide before using Go-to.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Recovery example text stating to remove and add the AGV before using Go-to.

stop_or_escalate_if: The remove-and-add procedure is not already known or available in the interface.
You do not have the required authorization to perform remove/add.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide example: if a tipper goes down and an AGV will not leave, remove and add the AGV and use Go-to to drop it off at the hospital.

steps.title: Select the Go to function
steps.purpose: Open the manual destination action used to send the AGV to a selected square.
steps.instruction: Select the Go to function for the AGV.
steps.expected_result: The Go to control is activated for the selected AGV.
steps.healthy_condition: The Go to option is available and can be selected for the AGV.
steps.failure_condition: The Go to option is not visible, not available, or cannot be selected.

actions.label: Open Go to
actions.exact_action: Select the Go to function for the AGV.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Go to control in the robot selection workflow.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Go to function is not present for the selected AGV.
The interface does not allow the Go to action.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Steps shown include select an AGV, select Go to, select which square the AGV should go to, and select Confirm.

steps.title: Select the hospital drop-off square
steps.purpose: Choose the destination square referenced by the recovery example.
steps.instruction: Select the square used for the hospital drop-off location, as referenced by the slide example.
steps.expected_result: The hospital drop-off square is selected as the AGV destination.
steps.healthy_condition: A valid square corresponding to the hospital drop-off area is identifiable and selected.
steps.failure_condition: The hospital drop-off square cannot be identified or selected.

actions.label: Choose destination square
actions.exact_action: Select which square you want the AGV to go to, using the hospital drop-off square for this recovery example.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Step on the slide indicating square selection for the Go to destination.

stop_or_escalate_if: The hospital drop-off square is not identifiable from the interface or local documentation.
The selected square does not appear to correspond to the hospital drop-off area.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide example references using Go-to to drop the AGV off at the hospital.
Steps shown include selecting which square the AGV should go to.

steps.title: Confirm the Go to destination
steps.purpose: Send the AGV to the selected hospital drop-off square.
steps.instruction: Select Confirm to send the AGV to the selected hospital drop-off square.
steps.expected_result: The Go to action is confirmed and the AGV is sent toward the selected hospital drop-off location.
steps.healthy_condition: The destination is accepted and the AGV is directed to the selected square.
steps.failure_condition: The confirmation cannot be completed or the AGV is not sent to the selected square.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Confirm step in the Go to workflow.

stop_or_escalate_if: The AGV does not accept or begin the directed move after confirmation.

steps.title: Remove the tote so the AGV can resume tasking
steps.purpose: Complete the source-described recovery outcome that allows the AGV to receive a new task.
steps.instruction: Remove the tote from the AGV, because the slide states the AGV will pick up a new task once its tote has been removed.
steps.expected_result: The tote is removed from the AGV and the AGV can pick up a new task.
steps.healthy_condition: After tote removal, the AGV picks up a new task.
steps.failure_condition: The tote cannot be removed or the AGV does not pick up a new task after tote removal.

actions.label: Remove tote
actions.exact_action: Remove the tote from the AGV.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Recovery example text stating the AGV will pick up a new task once its tote has been removed.

stop_or_escalate_if: The AGV does not pick up a new task after tote removal.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The AGV will pick up a new task once its tote has been removed.

success_criteria: The affected AGV is selected and AGV information is displayed.
The Go to function is used to send the AGV to the hospital drop-off square.
The tote is removed from the AGV.
The AGV picks up a new task after tote removal.
healthy_conditions: AGV information appears after selecting the AGV object.
The Go to control is available for the selected AGV.
The hospital drop-off square can be identified and selected.
The AGV accepts the confirmed destination.
The AGV picks up a new task after tote removal.
failure_conditions: The AGV cannot be selected or AGV information does not display.
The remove-and-add action is not known, available, or authorized.
The Go to function is unavailable.
The hospital drop-off square cannot be identified.
The AGV does not move to the selected destination after confirmation.
The AGV does not pick up a new task after tote removal.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the remove-and-add procedure is not already known or available in the interface.
Escalate if the hospital drop-off square is not identifiable from the available interface or local documentation.
Escalate if the AGV does not pick up a new task after tote removal.
Escalate for SME review because the source provides an example recovery but not a complete operational procedure for all actions.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary training slide for AGV Go-to recovery example, including AGV selection, Go to, square selection, Confirm, and hospital drop-off example.

missing_details: The source does not define the exact procedure for removing and adding the AGV.
The source does not identify the exact hospital drop-off square or how to determine it in the interface.
The source does not provide explicit permissions, approvals, or role boundaries for remove/add actions.
The source does not provide timing expectations for the AGV to arrive or pick up a new task.
